title: T-004 — site_settings table + L2 override pattern (§9.3, §13.2, ADR-031)
prompt:
# T-004 — site_settings table + L2 override + RLS site-scoped variant

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §9.3, §13.2 (ADR-031 schema variation per org)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Introduce `site_settings` backing L2 per-site config overrides (e.g., shift_pattern, fefo_strategy, default_currency, language). Site-scoped RLS using app.current_site_id() from T-001.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0043_site_settings.sql`:
   - CREATE TABLE site_settings per §9.3: id, org_id, site_id (FK sites), setting_key, setting_value JSONB, source CHECK ('default','l2_override'), updated_by, updated_at, UNIQUE(org_id, site_id, setting_key).
   - ENABLE + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
   - Policy `site_settings_scope` USING (org_id = app.current_org_id() AND (app.current_site_id() IS NULL OR site_id = app.current_site_id())) WITH CHECK same — site-scoped variant per §6.4 + §14.1.
   - CREATE INDEX idx_site_settings_lookup ON site_settings(org_id, site_id, setting_key).
2. Drizzle schema + types + zod schema with discriminated source enum.
3. Helper `getSiteSetting(orgId, siteId, key)` reads override-or-L1-fallback (Settings org_config table fallback wired in T-026).

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0043_site_settings.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/site-settings.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/site-settings.zod.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/site-settings.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given migration 0043 is applied, when \d site_settings inspected, then all 8 cols + unique(org_id,site_id,setting_key) + RLS+FORCE RLS present + source CHECK constraint enforced.
2. Given source value 'unknown' is inserted, when the row is committed, then DB raises check_violation.
3. Given two sites X and Y in same org each have a setting_key='fefo_strategy', when app_user is in withSiteContext(siteId=X), then SELECT returns only X's row.
4. Given super_admin context with app.current_site_id() IS NULL, when SELECT runs against site_settings, then both sites' rows are returned (ALL-sites mode for cross-site audit).
5. Given pg_policies for site_settings, when inspected, then policy references both app.current_org_id() AND app.current_site_id() — proves site-scoping variant.

## Test strategy
- RED: assert source CHECK enforcement + site-scoped RLS isolation + super_admin ALL-mode + pg_policies dual-reference grep.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration -- site-settings'.

## Risk red lines
- Do not omit org_id from policy USING — RLS must check both org and site (defense-in-depth).
- Always call app.current_site_id() helper — never read the raw GUC directly.
- Do not allow source='l2_override' rows without a matching L1 default at org_config — validation deferred to T-026 UI; do not enforce here.
- Do not skip FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
- Do not store secrets in setting_value JSONB; document this in scope notes.

out_of_scope: Do not implement the UI override modal here (T-026).
Do not seed L1 org_config defaults — owned by 02-SET §9.
Do not implement config promotion across levels (T-019 covers replication retry; promotion is P2 §10B MS-105).

cross_module_dependencies.reason: L1 org_config is the fallback; site_settings is the L2 override layer per ADR-031.
Cross-mod dep to 00-foundation T-125 declared (withOrgContext + app.current_org_id() per F11 rule). withSiteContext composes on top of T-125; all site-scoped tasks must carry this cross-module dep.

acceptance_criteria: Given migration 0043 is applied, when \\d site_settings inspected, then all 8 cols + unique(org_id,site_id,setting_key) + RLS+FORCE RLS + source CHECK constraint present.

Given source value 'unknown' is inserted, when the row is committed, then DB raises check_violation; AND given pg_policies for the table when inspected then policy references app.current_org_id() AND app.current_site_id() with no raw current_setting GUC reads.

Given two sites X and Y in same org each have a setting_key='fefo_strategy', when app_user is in withSiteContext(siteId=X), then SELECT returns only X's row; AND Given pg_policies for site_settings, when inspected, then policy references both app.current_org_id() AND app.current_site_id() — site-scoping variant.
Given super_admin context with app.current_site_id() IS NULL, when SELECT runs against site_settings, then both sites' rows are returned (ALL-sites mode).
test_strategy: RED: assert source CHECK + site-scoped RLS isolation + super_admin ALL-mode + pg_policies dual-reference grep.
risk_red_lines: Do not omit org_id from policy USING — RLS must check both (defense-in-depth).
Always call app.current_site_id() helper — never read the raw GUC directly.
Do not allow source='l2_override' rows without matching L1 default at org_config — UI validation deferred to T-026.
Do not skip FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
Do not store secrets in setting_value JSONB.
